title: IT quality and testing professionals
description: Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Plan, implement, upgrade, or monitor security measures for the protection of computer networks and information. Assess system vulnerabilities for security risks and propose and implement risk mitigation strategies. May ensure appropriate security controls are in place that will safeguard digital files and vital electronic infrastructure. May respond to computer security breaches and viruses. Design strategies for enterprise databases, data warehouse systems, and multidimensional networks. Set standards for database operations, programming, query processes, and security. Model, design, and construct large relational databases or data warehouses. Create and optimize data models for warehouse infrastructure and workflow. Integrate new systems with existing warehouse structure and refine system performance and functionality. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Train users and promote security awareness to ensure system security and improve server and network efficiency.
Monitor use of data files and regulate access to safeguard information in computer files.
Develop plans to safeguard computer files against unauthorized modification, destruction, or disclosure and meet emergency data processing needs.
Review violations of computer security procedures and discuss them with violators to prevent repetition.
Document computer security and emergency measures policies, procedures, and tests.
Perform risk assessments and test data processing systems to ensure functioning and security measures.
Encrypt data transmissions and erect firewalls to protect confidential information.
Coordinate implementation of computer system plans with staff and vendors.
Monitor reports of computer viruses to determine when to update protection systems.
Confer with users about computer data access needs, security violations, and programming changes.
Conduct software compatibility tests with programs, hardware, or network environments.
Collaborate with customers to evaluate problems and recommend solutions.
Identify program deviances from standards and suggest modifications for compliance.
Design or develop automated testing tools and coordinate user testing.
Document software defects using a bug tracking system and report defects to developers.
Perform initial debugging procedures by reviewing configuration files or logs to identify issues.
Create, analyze, report, and transfer data using specialized applications program software.
Develop testing programs that address database impacts, software scenarios, and usability.
Monitor program performance to ensure efficient operations and troubleshoot issues.
Document test procedures and develop standards to determine product quality or readiness.
Design test plans, scenarios, scripts, or procedures to ensure thorough testing.
Prepare and record changes to official documents and confirm with legal and compliance staff.
Oversee performance of risk assessment, conduct investigations of security breaches, and evaluate the damage to identify vulnerabilities.
Develop or implement software tools to assist in the detection, prevention, and analysis of security threats and recommend information security enhancements.
Monitor systems for intrusions, conduct network audits, and coordinate documentation of security policies, procedures, or tests.
Perform vulnerability scans and assessments using established criteria and maintain up-to-date knowledge of hacking trends and tools.
Provide technical support to computer users for security products and train staff on information security standards and best practices.
Identify security weaknesses through penetration tests and assess current systems to determine effectiveness against threats.
Review security assessments for compliance with standards and develop incident response strategies for security breaches.
Develop and document database architectures and manage database developments to ensure scalability, performance, and reliability.
Test software systems for security vulnerabilities, monitor system operations, and regularly evaluate hardware and software performance.
Collaborate with IT teams to discuss security solutions, evaluate emerging technologies, and implement security measures for systems.
Consult with multiple stakeholders to define requirements and implement features for projects.
Develop and manage project plans, including objectives, schedules, funding, and staffing.
Monitor project milestones and deliverables to ensure adherence to quality standards and budget.
Prepare project status reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing information and trends.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems and provide performance feedback.
Create or implement metadata processes and frameworks for data management.
Develop and implement data extraction procedures from systems like administration and billing.
Design, implement, or operate comprehensive data warehouse systems to optimize resource utilization according to customer requirements.
Perform risk assessments to develop strategies for managing security risks.
Create plans, test files, and scripts for data warehouse testing and provide specifications to quality assurance staff.
